title: Overloaded print functions for different data types

content.problemStatement:
In this example, we demonstrate how to use function overloading in C++ to calculate the area of different shapes. The `calculateArea()` function is overloaded to handle different numbers of arguments: one for a square and two for a rectangle. The function will return the area of the shape based on the user's input.

---

sampleTestCases.output:
Area of the square: 25
Area of the rectangle: 24
